title: Multiple-choice cloze
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: Marine biologists have recently uncovered surprising behaviours in deep-sea species. Early observations (0) suggested that life in the abyss was scarce, but new technologies have revealed ecosystems far more dynamic than previously imagined.||Some organisms appear to (1) energy by entering semi-dormant states, while others (2) nutrients from chemical reactions rather than sunlight. These adaptations allow them to (3) extreme pressure and near-freezing temperatures.||Researchers are now attempting to (4) how such species evolved. One theory proposes that environmental instability forced them to (5) unconventional survival strategies.||However, studying these creatures is challenging. Equipment failures and unpredictable currents often (6) expeditions. Scientists must therefore (7) their methods and develop tools capable of withstanding harsh conditions. Only then can they hope to (8) the mysteries of the deep ocean.

example.explanation: “Suggested” is the appropriate verb for early scientific observations.

questions.explanation: “Derive nutrients from” is idiomatic in biology.
“Trace how species evolved” is the correct academic phrasing.
“Hinder expeditions” is the most natural collocation.
“Refine methods” is the standard scientific collocation.
